segments.text: How helpful you have been to this feeble man that I am. How supportive you have been to the weak.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: What good advice you have given to this ignorant man, demonstrating you have so much wisdom.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Who helped you speak these words? Who inspired you to say such things?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The dead tremble, those beneath the waters.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Sheol lies naked before God, Abaddon is uncovered.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He stretches the northern sky over empty space; he hangs the world on nothing.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He gathers the rain in his storm clouds which do not break under the weight.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He veils his throne; covering it with his clouds.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: On the surface of the waters he set a boundary; he set a limit dividing light from darkness.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The pillars of heaven tremble; they shake with fear at his rebuke.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He calmed the sea with his power; because he knew what to do, he crushed Rahab.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The breath of his voice made the heavens beautiful; with his hand he pierced the gliding serpent.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: This is just a little of all he does—what we hear of him is hardly a whisper, so who can understand his thunderous power?
segments.speaker: Unknown
